task_completion.description: Verify that the required networking and coroutine dependencies were added to the app's build.gradle.kts file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for app/build.gradle.kts open in the editor. It should contain implementation lines for retrofit (2.9.0), converter-moshi (2.9.0), moshi-kotlin (1.15.0), logging-interceptor (4.12.0), and kotlinx-coroutines-android (1.7.3).

task_completion.description: Verify that the INTERNET permission was added to the AndroidManifest.xml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml open in the editor. It should contain the tag <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Post data model with the correct fields and Moshi annotation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Post.kt open in the editor. It should be a data class containing fields for id, userId, title, and body, and it must be annotated with @JsonClass(generateAdapter = true).

task_completion.description: Verify the PostApiService interface has the correct suspend functions and Retrofit annotations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for PostApiService.kt open in the editor. It should be an interface with three suspend functions: getPosts() with @GET("posts"), getPost(@Path("id") id: Int) with @GET("posts/{id}"), and getPostsByUser(@Query("userId") userId: Int) with @GET("posts").

task_completion.description: Verify the ApiClient singleton is configured with the correct base URL and logging interceptor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for ApiClient.kt open in the editor. It should show a Retrofit builder using the base URL 'https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/', an OkHttpClient added with an HttpLoggingInterceptor set to Level.BODY, and it should expose the PostApiService.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Android project builds successfully after the additions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Build' tool window in Android Studio showing 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or a terminal window showing a successful execution of a Gradle build command (e.g., ./gradlew build) with no compilation errors.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an IDE (like Android Studio) or a legitimate text editor to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a real editor interface (Android Studio, IntelliJ, VS Code, or nano/vim) rather than just echoing text directly into files from a script.

integrity.description: Verify the files were created in the correct package directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: The project explorer or file paths in the editor tabs should show the files located in app/src/main/java/com/example/postviewer/model/ and app/src/main/java/com/example/postviewer/network/.

integrity.description: Verify the build success message is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The build success message should appear in the standard Gradle build output window or terminal, not as a manually printed string or a text file named 'build_success.txt'.

The API base URL must be exactly https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/.
